name: Grip & Sip
address: 139 W MacArthur Dr, Cottage Hills, IL 62018
about:
Grip & Sip is an indoor entertainment venue that combines golf and pickleball with a full-service bar experience. The facility features three state-of-the-art golf simulators and six indoor pickleball courts, providing year-round recreation regardless of weather conditions. The venue caters to various skill levels, offering spaces for individual practice, friendly matches, and league play.

The atmosphere is designed for social interaction and relaxation, featuring a laid-back lounge area with multiple TVs, craft cocktails, and a selection of beers. Beyond casual play, Grip & Sip offers private party rentals and custom group packages for events ranging from corporate team building to birthday celebrations. The venue is locally owned and focuses on bringing recreation and connection to the Riverbend area.
why_golfers_like_it: High-end simulator technology with detailed feedback
Full-service bar and lounge with multiple TVs
Year-round indoor play in a climate-controlled facility
Private bay rentals for groups and events
No weather constraints for practice or play

food_and_drink.notes: Full-service bar with cocktails, beer, and spirits

simulator_hardware.notes: State of the art, full package simulators with best tech and feedback

amenities_list: Full-service bar|Pickleball courts|Multiple TVs|Private party rentals|Event space|Climate-controlled facility|Gift cards

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: While not explicitly stated on the website, most indoor golf venues provide rental clubs. It is recommended to contact Grip & Sip directly to confirm club availability.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: As a full-service bar venue, outside food and drinks are likely not permitted. Grip & Sip offers a selection of cocktails, beer, and spirits on-site.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: A standard round of 18 holes on a golf simulator typically takes about 45 to 60 minutes, depending on the skill level and pace of play.

faq.question: Is this kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Grip & Sip appears to be kid-friendly. The venue offers custom group packages suitable for birthdays and casual get-togethers, and simulators are generally accessible to younger players.
